summary: Manual smoke remains green, and MVP-fast Playwright production-preview smoke covers coordinates, HUD fit, nonblank canvas, assetBuild URLs, Pages routing, hold-fire aim/zoom, effect-pose alignment, pitch-corrected shot math, entry-splitter validation, movement collision, browser zoom guards, right-side control layout, and foundation texture loading/rendering.
observations: Local browser smoke used an 844 x 390 landscape viewport.
Debug API reported all three weapon asset IDs loaded with no assetLoadErrors.
VAULT selector click updated active weapon state and HUD label.
Fire button click changed VAULT ammo from 12 to 11 and incremented shotCount to 1.
Shared texture public URL returned 200 with image/png.
Repeated fire and a double-click path kept visualViewport.scale at 1.
SPARK lastShot reported blockedByWall true at tile [22, 1] with distance 20.5.
Fresh reload event check found no new Log.entryAdded or Runtime.exceptionThrown events.
Playwright smoke passed at 667x375, 740x360, 844x390, 932x430, and 1024x768.
Coordinate HUD text matched the debug player position format.
Weapon preview, GLB model, and shared texture resource URLs included the production build id as assetBuild.
The Playwright gate checks HUD group overlap and badge text overflow.
Latest validate:browser passed after closing six corner pinch tiles and adding weapon wall avoidance.
Unit coverage now checks the reported world coordinates and a weapon-only near-wall overlap case.
Latest validate:browser passed after widening structural entries to 5 tiles and adding shared weapon shot-effect pose math.
Playwright now holds fire and asserts ammo/shotCount changes by cadence plus centered-aim muzzle and aligned tracer/impact effect pose.
Latest validate:browser passed after adding structural entry splitter validation and widening row 30/39 split entries.
Unit coverage now pins the user-reported world coordinates near row 39/40 and checks the widened row slices.
Unit coverage now verifies body-only player collision, non-blocking weapon-only footprint overlap, wall depenetration, and tangential slide while pushed into a wall.
Latest Playwright smoke drives keyboard movement into the near-spawn top wall, asserts the player footprint stays clear during wall push, then escapes and moves east.
Unit coverage now verifies per-weapon muzzle offsets and pitch-corrected shot distance conversion.
Captured SPARK, BORE, and VAULT level/fire screenshots plus SPARK pitch-up/pitch-down firing screenshots under artifacts/sub-agents/20260704-weapon-framing/smoke-qa-agent.
Browser smoke now simulates a full-canvas double tap, a two-finger pinch gesture, WebKit-style gesture events, and a ctrl-wheel zoom gesture, then asserts visualViewport.scale stays at 1.
The debug API reports zoom-guard counters for double taps, multi-touch starts/movement, WebKit gestures, and wheel zoom.
Browser smoke holds move/look touches while firing to check that active pointer ownership remains stable.
MVP browser smoke still boots all five required landscape viewports, but only chromium-modern-phone-landscape runs the heavier movement route and active zoom/multitouch gesture checks.
Local npm run test:e2e measured about 19 seconds after the MVP split and two-worker Playwright config.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewports after taller walls, larger guns, hold-fire aim zoom, and reticle fire icon changes.
Browser smoke now holds fire long enough to verify multiple shots, ammo reduction, isFireHeld, positive aimBlend, and reduced cameraFovDegrees.
Browser smoke now waits for debug shot count cadence before releasing hold-fire, instead of relying on a fixed short hold duration.
Browser smoke verifies release cleanup by checking isFireHeld clears and shotCount stops increasing after release.
All viewports still prove the fire control produces a shot; multi-shot cadence, FOV/aim blend, and effect-pose detail checks stay scoped to chromium-modern-phone-landscape.

Browser smoke checks the fire button has no F text, the reticle icon is visible, the removed gun-cycle selector is absent, controls fit the viewport, touch targets are at least 44 px, and fire/stick controls do not overlap.
Browser smoke now expects three environment texture asset IDs and verifies their assetBuild URLs.
Browser smoke now waits for renderer texture count before validating the initial frame so colored foundation textures are uploaded, not just downloaded.
Screenshot QA confirmed the foundation reads as orange wall panels, a green tiled floor, and a purple ceiling at gameplay height.
Renderer budget assertions now include draw calls, triangles, geometries, and textures.
The heavy chromium-modern-phone-landscape project now runs a five-iteration QA restart loop and rechecks loaded asset IDs plus renderer budgets after each restart.
decisions: Keep manual browser smoke for this slice.
Automate smoke once browser test harness work starts.
Track visualViewport.scale for double-tap zoom regressions.
Full playthrough QA remains a separate gate.
Keep Playwright running against the Pages preview path rather than the dev root path.
Keep the hold-fire effect-pose browser assertion as a regression gate.
Keep the keyboard movement route as the current browser regression for body collision and wall push behavior.
Add deterministic close-wall pose/input coverage for snapshot.weapon.wallAvoidance in a later smoke slice.
Add weapon switching and fast-fire/no-zoom assertions in a later smoke slice.
Use snapshot.weapon.effectPose as the automated tracer alignment signal because the primitive tracer is brief in screenshots.
Keep an initial viewport-scale assertion before movement-route smoke, then run active gesture smoke after deterministic movement so look-zone drags cannot rotate the route setup.
Use two Playwright workers for MVP browser smoke while keeping the heavier route on one viewport to avoid repeating the same WebGL interaction path five times.
Treat hold-fire as the canonical automated firing path.
Keep reticle/control-fit checks in all viewports because the right-side action pad remains a thumb-zone risk.
caughtIssues: Dev-browser FPS hovered around 57 during first weapon smoke and around 56 during the latest fast-fire smoke.
Plain vite preview did not emulate the GitHub Pages project base path for built asset URLs.
Headless Chromium can emit empty Three shader validation messages after multiple WebGL contexts; the smoke filters only that known empty-log validation noise.
Close-wall viewmodel retraction is currently unit/debug-state visible but not yet asserted by Playwright look interaction.
Pitch-up and pitch-down screenshot QA currently uses pointer-drag approximation because deterministic debug look poses do not exist yet.
Previous double-tap smoke covered repeated fire/UI controls but not the full look surface or pinch gestures.
Repeating full movement/gesture smoke across all five viewports made the Pages workflow slow for MVP-level coverage.
Earlier fire smoke only proved one shot and did not catch hold cadence, release cleanup, FOV zoom, or weapon-centering state.
risks: Without automated browser smoke, future asset 404 regressions can still reach manual QA.
FPS should be checked in production preview before heavier asset imports.
Physical mobile Safari should still be used for final double-tap and pinch confidence because headless Chromium cannot fully emulate browser chrome.
Held-fire visual framing for SPARK, BORE, and VAULT still needs screenshot QA on real mobile or deterministic debug poses.
Full-route coverage should be re-expanded per viewport before a larger release gate or when viewport-specific gameplay bugs appear.
